topic: Thalassemia — emergency and acute care (knowledge base)
disclaimer: Educational prototype only. Not medical advice. Clinicians must follow local protocols and official WHO guidance.

who_pdf_links.title: WHO IRIS repository (search: thalassemia, transfusion, blood safety)

structured_rules.summary: Suspected acute severe anemia or hemodynamic compromise in a known or suspected thalassemia patient.
signals: Hb not available but clinical shock
structured_rules.action: Emergency evaluation, oxygen as indicated, IV access, labs including FBC and reticulocytes, consider transfusion per protocol.

structured_rules.summary: Chronic transfusion programs require monitoring for iron overload; acute presentations may overlap with infection or cardiac strain.
structured_rules.action: Specialist review; evaluate cardiac and endocrine status per guideline pathways; do not change chelation without specialist input.

structured_rules.summary: Functional hyposplenism or splenectomy increases infection risk; fever can be an emergency in vulnerable patients.
structured_rules.action: Sepsis pathway per local guideline; early antibiotics if indicated; notify hematology.
